title: AddTables result in Removed Records: AutoFilter from /xl/tables/table1.xml part (Table) when header row not displayed
author: GeeWee
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

I create a table with the following code (there is nothing else in the worksheet):
```typescript
// This does not work
  const table = worksheet.addTable({
    name: 'MyName',
    ref: 'A1',
    headerRow: false,
    columns: [
      { name: 'foo' },
      { name: 'bar' },
      { name: 'qux' },
    ],
    rows: [
      [1, 2, 3]
    ],
  });
  table.commit();
```

When opened in Excel it tells me there are errors and asks me whether or not I want to recover the document.
The full error log is as follows:
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<recoveryLog xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main"><logFileName>error109280_01.xml</logFileName>
<summary>Errors were detected in file 'C:\Users\Wengel\WebstormProjects\unihelper-monorepo\test-file.xlsx'</summary>
<removedRecords><removedRecord>Removed Records: AutoFilter from /xl/tables/table1.xml part (Table)</removedRecord></removedRecords>
</recoveryLog>
```

When excel recovers the table it displays fine.

It seems like the same type of error as in #1122 

**Lib version: 4.2.0**

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

It seems to me like this *only* happens when `headerRow` is set to false. If I take the same code above and change `headerRow` to be true (see following snippet) the **error does not occur and the file opens normally**

```typescript
// This works fine
  const table = worksheet.addTable({
    name: 'MyName',
    ref: 'A1',
    headerRow: true,
    columns: [
      { name: 'foo' },
      { name: 'bar' },
      { name: 'qux' },
    ],
    rows: [
      [1, 2, 3]
    ],
  });
  table.commit();
```
#

comments.author: XuJaven
comments.body:
At first,I found it will error when the columns has  two same name😥
then,I let its header not displayed but it ticket new error😥

comments.author: Gastonite
alexhalf
comments.body: The same issue is happened also if you merge cells a1:a2

comments.author: nbelyh
comments.body:
This seems to be the reason for this bug:
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/2ab468b8bae2c8d5844f922d61ac7d960fee26be/lib/xlsx/xform/table/table-xform.js#L45

Most probably should be:
```js
  headerRowCount: model.headerRow ? '1' : undefined,
```

comments.author: juliofihdeldev
comments.body: Did someone find an alternative for that ?

comments.author: jihun-ko
comments.body: For me, the issue was naming table name with hyphen(-). DO NOT NAME THE TABLE NAME WITH HYPHEN. NAME IT CAMELCASE INSTEAD.
